title: Sandbox Tauri IPC: kernel.sandbox.list_adapters + capabilities(adapter_id) + bootstrap registry wiring
goal: Expose the registered SandboxAdapter set + their AdapterCapabilities to the frontend via Tauri IPC per spec §3.5.4 ('the frontend reads via Tauri IPC'). Also lands the application bootstrap that constructs the registry, registers WSL2PodmanAdapter (default) + WindowsNativeJailAdapter (if MT-046 unblocked) + DockerAdapter (registered but not opt-in), and stores the registry in Tauri app state for the IPC handlers.
expected_diff_shape: New Tauri command module app/src-tauri/src/commands/sandbox.rs exposing `kernel_sandbox_list_adapters` and `kernel_sandbox_capabilities`; bootstrap addition in app/src-tauri/src/main.rs (or lib.rs) constructing SandboxAdapterRegistry and managing it via `tauri::Manager::manage`; new src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/bootstrap.rs that builds the canonical registry for both Tauri runtime and integration tests. Frontend type stubs under app/src/components/diagnostics/sandbox/types.ts (TS interface mirroring AdapterCapabilities).
unblock_conditions: Trait, registry, and all three adapter implementations merged. If MT-046 is BLOCKED (license escalation), MT-048 may proceed with WindowsNativeJailAdapter registration commented out + a TODO referencing the MT-045 decision record.
implementation_notes: bootstrap.rs: `pub fn build_default_registry() -> Result<SandboxAdapterRegistry, SandboxAdapterError>` that (1) constructs WSL2PodmanAdapter (best-effort; if WSL2 not present, log warning + skip registration but continue), (2) constructs WindowsNativeJailAdapter on Windows (cfg target_os; on non-Windows, skip), (3) constructs DockerAdapter (best-effort; if docker CLI missing, skip), (4) calls SandboxAdapterRegistry::new(default_adapter_id="wsl2_podman") and registers each constructed adapter, (5) reads `settings.sandbox.docker_explicit_opt_in` and calls set_docker_explicit_opt_in. If WSL2PodmanAdapter cannot be constructed AND no fallback adapter is available, log loud and return SandboxAdapterError::AdapterUnavailable (Handshake refuses to start without a default sandbox per spec §3.6.2). Tauri commands (app/src-tauri/src/commands/sandbox.rs): `#[tauri::command] async fn kernel_sandbox_list_adapters(registry: tauri::State<'_, Arc<SandboxAdapterRegistry>>) -> Result<Vec<AdapterCapabilities>, String>` returns `registry.list()` serialized; `#[tauri::command] async fn kernel_sandbox_capabilities(adapter_id: String, registry: tauri::State<'_, Arc<SandboxAdapterRegistry>>) -> Result<AdapterCapabilities, String>` returns `registry.get(&AdapterId(adapter_id)).map(|a| a.capabilities())`. Wire both into the Tauri builder's invoke_handler in main.rs. Frontend TS types in app/src/components/diagnostics/sandbox/types.ts mirror the AdapterCapabilities struct exactly (IsolationStrength, GpuPassthrough, ThroughputClass enums as TS union types). Add a tiny app/src/components/diagnostics/sandbox/SandboxAdapterList.tsx (or .vue per app stack — check existing app/src layout) that calls `invoke('kernel_sandbox_list_adapters')` and renders a read-only list — this is the Diagnostics-panel hook used later by cluster X. Tests: integration test that boots a fake Tauri context (per Tauri 2.x test-utils pattern) and invokes both commands; assertion that at least one adapter is registered AND default exists; assertion that capabilities(unknown_id) returns a typed error (mapped to a String per Tauri command convention but the error variant is structured).
risk_if_missed: Without IPC, the frontend (Diagnostics Panel + ModelRuntime Control Panel cluster X surfaces) cannot read AdapterCapabilities, breaking AC-SANDBOX-CAP-DECL and downstream cluster X work; without bootstrap, the registry exists in code but is never instantiated at runtime.
validator_focus: (a) Bootstrap survives missing WSL2 (skips registration, logs loud) but refuses to start with zero adapters; (b) Tauri commands serialize AdapterCapabilities to JSON; (c) Frontend TS types compile against the JSON shape (`tsc --noEmit` on the types file); (d) Docker is registered but selection.select() still refuses Docker without explicit opt-in.

dependency_override.override_basis: MT-048 unblock_conditions allow work to proceed when MT-046 is BLOCKED by license/escalation, with WindowsNativeJailAdapter registration skipped and a TODO/reference to the MT-045 decision record.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Refuse-to-start test: bootstrap with zero adapters registered MUST return AdapterUnavailable, not a partially-functional registry.
TS types compile check (`tsc --noEmit` on types.ts) so frontend cannot drift from backend AdapterCapabilities shape.
Tauri-command unknown-adapter test returns a typed error rather than panicking inside the command handler.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Bootstrap, Tauri IPC commands, and frontend TS types are landed and wired into the Tauri builder; refuse-to-start is tested; LoopbackOnly silently mapped to --network=none in the registered podman/docker adapters carries over here because bootstrap is the user of those adapters.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Bootstrap, Tauri IPC commands, and frontend TS types are landed and wired into the Tauri builder; refuse-to-start is tested; LoopbackOnly silently mapped to --network=none in the registered podman/docker adapters carries over here because bootstrap is the user of those adapters.
